title: Major Lithium Mine Approved in Nevada in Latest Effort to Support a Domestic Supply of Critical Minerals
content: The U.S. Department of the Interior announced the approval of the Rhyolite Ridge Lithium-Boron mining project in Nevada, a key step towards strengthening domestic critical mineral supply chains. The project, led by Ioneer, will supply lithium to power nearly 370,000 electric vehicles annually, create hundreds of jobs, and support clean energy goals. The Bureau of Land Management (BLM) collaborated with stakeholders to develop protections for the endangered Tiehm's buckwheat plant, and the mine will generate significant local economic benefits, including job training and scholarships. Boron will also be produced for various industrial applications.
author: Analysts Account
